Biblically Responsible Investing

Invest like it all belongs to God.

Because it does. Every dollar you steward is an opportunity to reflect your faith and honor Him.

At Northeast Planning Associates, we help Christians align their portfolios with their faith, so your money can reflect the Kingdom you’re praying for.

Why It Matters

Would you want your investments supporting what you pray against? Most Christians have simply never been given the choice.

Most Christians have never seen what their mutual funds actually own. Behind the ticker symbols are real companies doing real things — some that advance human flourishing, and some that may conflict with the values you hold most deeply.

Biblically Responsible Investing (BRI) closes that gap. It’s the modern application of an ancient truth: we are stewards, not owners. The money in your accounts was entrusted to you by God — and how you deploy it is worship, not just math.

“The earth is the Lord’s, and everything in it.”  — Psalm 24:1 (NASB 2020)

How BRI Works

Three ways your money can honor God

BRI isn’t just about what you avoid. It’s a proactive strategy to put your capital to work for good.

1

Avoid

Thoughtfully screen out companies whose primary business conflicts with biblical values, such as abortion, pornography, and addiction.

2

Affirm

Invest with conviction in companies that serve people, strengthen families, and reflect biblical values in how they operate.

3

Advocate

Use shareholder engagement to be a positive voice for biblical values where business decisions are made.

And no, faith and returns are not a trade-off. Leading biblically screened funds have delivered performance in line with the S&P 500.3

Curious what your money is supporting right now?

Most investors have never looked inside their own funds. One free conversation can change that.

“For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.”  — Matthew 6:21 (NASB 2020)

Book a Free Discovery Meeting
Our Exclusion Filters

Values you can actually see

We focus our screens where the leading biblically responsible fund families — Inspire, Timothy Plan, GuideStone, Eventide, Ave Maria — agree.4 Four core convictions, applied to every holding:

Sanctity of Life

Companies that perform, facilitate, or profit from the ending of unborn life — screened by every major biblically responsible fund manager.

Abortion providersAbortifacient drugsEmbryonic stem-cell research

“For You created my innermost parts; You wove me in my mother’s womb.” — Psalm 139:13

Pornography & Exploitation

Companies that produce or distribute pornography, or profit from the exploitation of people made in God’s image.

Adult contentSexually explicit mediaHuman trafficking ties

“I will set no worthless thing before my eyes.” — Psalm 101:3

Addictive Products

Companies whose business model depends on addiction — profiting when people are mastered by something other than Christ.

Gambling & casinosTobacco & nicotineCannabisAlcohol production

“All things are permitted for me, but I will not be mastered by anything.” — 1 Corinthians 6:12

Degrading Entertainment

Media and entertainment companies whose content is overtly violent, immoral, or corrosive to families.

Explicit mediaGratuitous violenceAnti-family content

“Whatever is true, whatever is honorable, whatever is right… think about these things.” — Philippians 4:8

Screened with intention — and tailored to your convictions

These four pillars are where the biblically responsible investing industry agrees, and every holding is reviewed against them. Beyond the core, your screens can flex: some families also choose to exclude weapons manufacturers, predatory lending, or other concerns close to their hearts.

Common Questions

Answers before you even ask

Will I sacrifice returns by investing biblically?

It’s the most common question we hear — and a fair one. Values-aligned portfolios are built with the same discipline around diversification, cost, and risk as any portfolio we manage, and some leading biblically screened funds have performed in line with broad market benchmarks (see the sources in the footer). That said, past performance is not indicative of future results, and any screening approach can perform differently than the broad market.

What exactly is a Biblical Portfolio X-Ray?

A live review of your current holdings during your free discovery meeting. We look inside the funds you already own, show you which companies and activities your dollars are supporting, and walk through what a values-aligned alternative could look like. There’s no cost, and the findings are yours to keep whether or not we ever work together.

Do I have to move all my accounts to get started?

No. Many families start with a single account — an old 401(k), an IRA, or a brokerage account — and expand from there only if it’s a good fit for them.

How much does it cost?

Discovery meetings are always free — take as many as you need. If we end up working together, fees depend on the services you choose, and we’ll explain them clearly, in plain English and in writing, before you commit to anything.

Do I have to be a Christian to work with you?

Not at all. We serve anyone who wants thoughtful, values-aligned planning. We simply specialize in helping Christians whose deepest desire is for their portfolio to reflect their faith.

We don’t live near Newington — can we still work with you?

Yes. We’re based on the New Hampshire Seacoast and meet with families in person across NH and New England — and virtually with clients around the country. Distance is never the obstacle.

Individual managers’ screens vary; not every manager screens every category listed.

Securities and advisory services offered through LPL Financial, a registered investment advisor.

Biblically Responsible Investing involves investment considerations and screening criteria that may differ from traditional investment strategies and may impact diversification and performance. No strategy assures success or protects against loss. All investing involves risk, including the potential loss of principal.
